title: Advisory Remediation Progress Tracking

compliance.assurance_rationale: Tracks Cisco PSIRT security advisories affecting managed devices, providing evidence of vulnerability awareness and remediation tracking as required by NIST 800-53 Flaw Remediation.
compliance.controlObjective: Demonstrates continuous monitoring of vendor-published security advisories for all managed network infrastructure.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search over index=catalyst sourcetype=cisco:dnac:securityadvisory scheduled daily; advisory counts and affected device lists exported to compliance dashboard.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Contributes to vulnerability scanning by tracking known vendor-published vulnerabilities, though it does not replace active scanning tools.
compliance.controlObjective: Provides a vendor-authoritative vulnerability feed for network infrastructure to complement active vulnerability scanning.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Dashboard panel showing advisory severity distribution and affected device counts, refreshed with each TA poll cycle.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Tracks remediation progress of technical vulnerabilities by comparing current vs historical affected device counts.
compliance.controlObjective: Demonstrates that identified vulnerabilities are being actively remediated over time.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Trending report showing advisory affected-device counts decreasing over 30-day windows; exported monthly for management review.

dataSources: index=catalyst, sourcetype cisco:dnac:securityadvisory (advisoryId, severity, deviceId, advisoryTitle; compare windows with earliest/latest)
spl: index=catalyst sourcetype="cisco:dnac:securityadvisory" | stats dc(deviceId) as current_affected by advisoryId, severity, advisoryTitle | appendcols [search index=catalyst sourcetype="cisco:dnac:securityadvisory" earliest=-30d latest=-7d | stats dc(deviceId) as previous_affected by advisoryId] | eval remediation_pct=if(previous_affected>0, round((previous_affected-current_affected)*100/previous_affected,1), "N/A") | where current_affected > 0 | sort severity -current_affected
description: Tracks the remediation progress of security advisories over time by comparing current affected device counts against historical baselines.
value: Vulnerability remediation is a process, not a one-time event. Tracking progress demonstrates active management and identifies stalled remediation efforts.
implementation: Enable the `securityadvisory` input. `appendcols` requires aligned `advisoryId` order between the two `stats` outputs; for production, consider a `join` on `advisoryId` or `lookup` of precomputed baselines. Validate that subsearch time bounds cover representative weeks.
visualization: Table (advisoryId, severity, current_affected, previous_affected, remediation_pct), column chart of remediation_pct.
knownFalsePositives: `appendcols` can **misalign** `advisoryId` rows if the subsearch returns a different **row order** than the main search, producing **nonsense** or **N/A** `remediation_pct`. **Dropping** device inventory (RMA, decommission) lowers `current_affected` and looks like **100%** patching even when you only removed assets. A **Catalyst** **re-sync** that **re-keys** `deviceId` can swing counts without a **single** **IOS** upgrade.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Cisco Catalyst Add-on (7538) with `cisco:dnac:securityadvisory` in `index=catalyst`.
‚Ä¢ For production, prefer `| join type=left advisoryId` (or a KVStore baseline from two scheduled searches) instead of `appendcols`, because `appendcols` only lines up by **row number** and can pair the wrong `advisoryId` with the wrong `previous_affected` when sort order differs.
‚Ä¢ ‚ÄúCurrent‚Äù uses the user‚Äôs time range; the inline subsearch uses a fixed **baseline** of `earliest=-30d latest=-7d`‚Äîtune to your change cadence.
‚Ä¢ `docs/implementation-guide.md`.

Step 1 ‚Äî Prototype in lab
‚Ä¢ Run the main and baseline `stats` alone; confirm both return the **same** `advisoryId` set and order before trusting `appendcols`.

Step 2 ‚Äî Remediation % (stalled work)
```spl
index=catalyst sourcetype="cisco:dnac:securityadvisory" | stats dc(deviceId) as current_affected by advisoryId, severity, advisoryTitle | appendcols [search index=catalyst sourcetype="cisco:dnac:securityadvisory" earliest=-30d latest=-7d | stats dc(deviceId) as previous_affected by advisoryId] | eval remediation_pct=if(previous_affected>0, round((previous_affected-current_affected)*100/previous_affected,1), "N/A") | where current_affected > 0 | sort severity -current_affected
```

Understanding this SPL
**Advisory Remediation Progress** ‚Äî Percent change in distinct affected `deviceId` between windows. Inventory shrink (RMA, decommission, site removed from Catalyst) can reduce `current_affected` without a single IOS upgrade, so do not use this as proof of patch alone; pair with change tickets and SWIM or fixed-version fields from Cisco where available.

**Pipeline walkthrough**
‚Ä¢ `stats` for current `dc(deviceId)` by `advisoryId` + baseline subsearch for `previous_affected` ‚Üí `remediation_pct` ‚Üí keep rows still open (`current_affected > 0`).

Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
‚Ä¢ For one `advisoryId`, hand-check current vs previous counts; if the percentage does not match simple arithmetic, switch to an explicit `join` on `advisoryId`.
‚Ä¢ Reconcile to Catalyst‚Äôs PSIRT view of remaining affected where the UI exposes it.

Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
‚Ä¢ Export monthly for GRC; flag advisories with low `remediation_pct` for multiple consecutive months as ‚Äústalled‚Äù in the vulnerability queue.

Step 5 ‚Äî Troubleshooting
‚Ä¢ **N/A** for `remediation_pct`: new advisory in current window (no `previous_affected` in baseline) or baseline subsearch had no data‚Äîwiden the baseline or accept N/A for brand-new items.
‚Ä¢ **Optimistic % after inventory cleanup:** document on the dashboard that `deviceId` count drops can reflect **scope** change, not only patching.
grandmaExplanation: We track how quickly your team is fixing security vulnerabilities by comparing how many devices are still affected compared to last month.
